steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Find all three angles in each triangle
steps.content: Angles in a triangle sum to $180^\circ$. Triangle GHI has $I=180^\circ-52^\circ-83^\circ=45^\circ$. Triangle MNO has $O=180^\circ-52^\circ-83^\circ=45^\circ$.

steps.title: Apply the AA similarity criterion
steps.content: Two triangles are similar when two pairs of corresponding angles are equal. The problem already gives $G=M=52^\circ$ and $H=N=83^\circ$, so AA similarity is established immediately.

steps.title: Choose the statement
steps.content: Because the two given angle pairs are enough, no additional information is needed. That matches Choice D — No additional information is needed..
